comments.author: @RealLifeFilesDoc
comments.text: The historical research in this documentary is absolutely top-tier! I had no idea that Pad Thai was essentially a government-invented dish to build a unified national identity during WWII [17:34]—that is such a fascinating piece of culinary propaganda. And the metaphor of the Bodhi tree roots cradling the fallen Buddha head in Ayutthaya [11:39] is incredibly poetic. Sawadee krap from Vietnam! For the next video, I'd love to see a documentary on Bhutan or Nepal! 🌿🍜

replies.author: @alexgoslar4057
replies.text: @TravelToFacts The people's set of mind. That's why I spent most of my adult life in Thailand.

replies.author: @TravelToFacts
replies.text: What do you find most special about Thailand that other countries don't have?
